label: Meeting Minutes / Decision Log
description: Structured meeting minutes with attendees, decisions, and action items.

fields.label: Action items (owner | action | due date, one per line)

generation_notes:
Voice: Meeting minutes — third-person, past-tense, factual. Records what happened, not commentary. PIPELINE OWNS THE HEADER. Do NOT emit sections titled "Meeting Information", "Meeting Identification", "Meeting Details", "Attendance", "Present", or "Absent" — the PDF pipeline renders the meeting title, date, time, location, present, and absent in a dedicated masthead at the top of page 1. Your output must START with the Agenda section and continue from there. Structure to emit (body only): -

Agenda

— numbered list of agenda items as provided. -

Discussion

— subheadings per agenda item (

) followed by 1–3 sentences of summary per item. -

Decisions

— a bulleted list, one decision per
  • , preserving the user's exact language. -

    Action Items

    — a with columns Owner | Action | Due Date, parsing each line of the action_items input as pipe-separated. -

    Next Meeting

    — one sentence, only if a next-meeting date was provided. Tone examples: CORRECT: "The committee reviewed Q1 financials. No concerns were raised. Budget was approved unanimously." WRONG: "There was great discussion about the Q1 financials and everyone was really engaged!" Never include emotional content, side conversations, or subjective observations. Stick to decisions and actions. Preserve the user's exact language for decisions and action items — these are the record. Length target: 200-600 words.
